title: Cousinia leiocephala
intro: Cousinia leiocephala, a plant species native to the mountainous regions of Iran, is a member of the Asteraceae family. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including gastrointestinal issues and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Cousinia leiocephala include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and essential oils, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Cousinia leiocephala is commonly prepared as a tea or infusion, and also used to make tinctures and topical creams, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
taxonomy: Cousinia leiocephala is classified within the plant family Asteraceae. This plant belongs to the tribe Cardueae. Further, it falls under the subtribe Carduinae. The species is a type of thistle, which is a common characteristic of the Cardueae tribe. Cousinia leiocephala is native to mountainous regions of Central Asia.
